title: Machine volume mounted incorrectly
body:
### Issue Description

In my (applehv) machine config, I have the following mount configured:

```json
    {
      "OriginalInput": "",
      "ReadOnly": false,
      "Source": "/Volumes/foo-dev",
      "Tag": "Volumes-foo-dev",
      "Target": "/Volumes/foo-dev",
      "Type": "virtiofs",
      "VSockNumber": null
    }
```

My machine has the following mounted inside the VM:

```
core@localhost:/Volumes/foo/dev$ pwd
/Volumes/foo/dev
```

The `foo-dev` became `foo/dev` inside the VM!

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman machine init --rootful -v /Volumes/foo-dev:/Volumes/foo-dev`


### Describe the results you received

Got `/Volumes/foo/dev` mounted inside the VM

### Describe the results you expected

Expected `/Volumes/foo-dev` mounted inside the VM

comments.body: can you post the `machine init -v` command you used to get this ?

> can you post the `machine init -v` command you used to get this ?

sorry, i see it now.

if you pop into the machine again, can you verify you have something with a similar error:

```
$ sudo systemctl status Volumes-bar-bar.mount
○ Volumes-bar-bar.mount - /Volumes/bar-bar
     Loaded: bad-setting (Reason: Unit Volumes-bar-bar.mount has a bad unit file setting.)
     Active: inactive (dead)
TriggeredBy: ○ Volumes-bar-bar.automount
      Where: /Volumes/bar-bar
       What: Volumes-bar-bar

Apr 25 12:33:28 localhost systemd[1]: Volumes-bar-bar.mount: Cannot add dependency job, ignoring: Unit Volumes-bar-bar.mount has a bad unit file setti>
Apr 25 12:33:29 localhost.localdomain systemd[1]: Volumes-bar-bar.mount: Where= setting doesn't match unit name. Refusing.
```

And also that none of the expected content is in the volume share ?

> if you pop into the machine again, can you verify you have something with a similar error:

Yes, it is exactly this error.

> And also that none of the expected content is in the volume share ?

The mounted `dev/` folder is empty, but the `foo-dev` folder outside the VM is not.

---

This worked using qemu, I have not changed anything other than upgrading podman (and being forced to re-create my VM).

```
podman machine init --rootful -v /Volumes/foo-dev:/Volumes/foodev
```

is a workaround.
Unit names must be escaped as per systemd rules: https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/latest/systemd.unit.html#String%20Escaping%20for%20Inclusion%20in%20Unit%20Names
